text: Agile methodology is a popular approach to project management, especially in software development. Instead of planning everything at the start, teams work in short cycles called sprints, usually lasting two weeks. At the beginning of each sprint, the team selects tasks from a backlog, which is a prioritized list of work items known as user stories. Every day, team members hold a quick standup meeting to discuss progress and any problems. This iterative development process allows teams to review and improve their work after each sprint, making it easier to adapt to changes and deliver value consistently.
